reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly on Content Accuracy. Instead of only reading the target sentence within the single quotes, it reads part of the prompt instructions aloud ('Initially, gradually transitioning to an enthusiastic tone') before beginning the actual script. This is a major hallucination/instruction-following error. Regarding the paralinguistic task, the tone during the actual script segment starts somewhat neutral rather than distinctly 'cold'. There is a slight increase in pitch and energy towards the end ('truly amazing'), but the transition is not particularly smooth or dramatic. The inclusion of the prompt text severely impacts the usability of the audio.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 performs excellently across all dimensions. Content Accuracy is perfect; it reads only the designated text. In terms of Paralinguistics, the model effectively captures the 'Dynamic Adjustment'. At 0:00-0:04 ('I didn't have high expectations...'), the voice adopts a lower pitch, slightly vocal-fry-heavy, and dismissive tone, effectively conveying the required 'cold' or uninterested attitude. Around 0:05 ('But after seeing him...'), the energy visibly lifts. From 0:06 to the end ('...truly amazing, it completely exceeded my imagination!'), the tone transforms into high-pitched, bright enthusiasm. The transition is natural and clearly discernible.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 committed a critical error by reading the prompt instructions ('Initially, gradually transitioning...') as part of the speech, which invalidates the result for practical use. Furthermore, Model 1's emotional range was limited; the 'cold' section sounded merely neutral, and the 'enthusiastic' section was restrained. Model 2, in contrast, followed the content instructions perfectly and demonstrated a strong, natural acting performance. The contrast between the bored/cold opening and the excited conclusion in Model 2 was distinct and adhered strictly to the dynamic adjustment request.
